claim_status: No-claim IPC wire-source observations only; external format specifications are not a selected encoding, generator approval, transport authentication, production IPC, renderer security, or implementation evidence.

sources.title: Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR)
sources.observation: CBOR defines a typed binary data model and deterministic-encoding guidance, but legal representations and generic values still require an application profile and bounds policy.
sources.decision_consequence: A Turing CBOR candidate must specify deterministic profile, tags, map policy, frame limits, allocation limits, and unknown-value behavior before comparison or approval.

sources.title: Protocol Buffers encoding
sources.observation: Protocol Buffers uses field numbers and wire types with length-delimited values, making schema and decoder policy central to size, recursion, unknown-field, and evolution behavior.
sources.decision_consequence: A Turing Protocol Buffers candidate must bound length-delimited and nested data, define unknown-field retention/rejection, audit generated runtime behavior, and keep capability meaning outside page-controlled fields.

sources.title: Protocol Buffers language specification
sources.observation: The language specification defines schema constructs and compatibility-relevant field semantics, but it does not authenticate peers or enforce Turing capabilities.
sources.decision_consequence: Schema evolution and compatibility must be reviewed separately from transport identity, capability authorization, resource accounting, and hostile-decoder behavior.

sources.title: FlatBuffers schema language
sources.observation: FlatBuffers provides a schema-driven representation and direct buffer access model, which shifts safety obligations to validation of offsets, lengths, alignment, nesting, strings, vectors, unions, and lifetimes.
sources.decision_consequence: A Turing FlatBuffers candidate must prove bounds-before-use and ownership/lifetime rules; zero-copy access cannot be counted as validation or peer authentication.

sources.title: FlatBuffers schema evolution
sources.observation: FlatBuffers documents additive evolution and deprecated-field practices, but application authority, version rejection, and resource policy remain Turing protocol responsibilities.
sources.decision_consequence: A candidate experiment must test incompatible fields, variants, capability meanings, document epochs, and resource-owner changes rather than assuming schema evolution preserves authority.

unsupported: No IPC encoding has been selected.
No schema generator or serialization dependency has been approved.
No authenticated platform transport, hostile-wire experiment, or owner-reviewed PB-011 readiness exists.
No renderer-security, process-isolation, site-isolation, production IPC, or implementation claim exists.
